title: PUBLIC HEALTH EMERGENCY PREPAREDNESS - 211info
text: GRANT COUNTY HEALTH DEPARTMENT PUBLIC HEALTH EMERGENCY PREPAREDNESS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Emergency Management Program works to minimize the effects of major emergencies and disasters on the community through: -Assessing community hazards and risks -Promoting and supporting preparedness activities -Developing disaster response and recovery plans -Training staff and conducting exercises to evaluate plans and systems -Building partnerships and coordinating plans with public agencies and private organizations -Maintaining an emergency operations center, emergency notification systems and public safety communication systems to coordinate emergency response efforts -Applying for and implementing emergency preparedness grants -Coordinating disaster assistance programs 528 E Main Street Suite E John Day, OR 97845 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 575-0429 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (541) 575-0429 Main phone Main phone (541) 575-0429 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website for more information LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Grant County Services: Disaster Preparedness Information Other Services Offered At This Location: CONSUMER REGULATION - VITAL RECORDS GENERAL MEDICAL CARE - GRANT COUNTY HEALTH DEPARTMENT RURAL HEALTH CLINIC HEALTH SCREENING/DIAGNOSTIC SERVICES - SCREENWISE-ENROLLING HEALTH SCREENING/DIAGNOSTIC SERVICES - STD CLINIC HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - OREGON MOTHERSCARE HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - PUBLIC HEALTH MODERNIZATION HUMAN REPRODUCTION - OREGON REPRODUCTIVE HEALTH PROGRAM CLINIC INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - BABIES FIRST! INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - CACOON INFORMATION SERVICES - TOBACCO PREVENTION AND EDUCATION PROGRAM PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - WOMEN, INFANTS, AND CHILDREN SUPPLEMENTAL NUTRITION PROGRAM SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - TUBERCULOSIS PROGRAM SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - VACCINES AND IMMUNIZATION If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
